title: Scholarship essays — OSU/Renaissance/Ford/JCEF + parallel hustle stack
html:
Scholarship essays — OSU/Renaissance/Ford/JCEF + parallel hustle stack

Source: 2020-11-15 Apple Note. 18-year-old senior Yahir's mid-November to-do block: four scholarship/college applications stacked above a five-track hustle plan (stock positions, Amazon income, marketing income, a DoorDash play for dad, and pre-departure adventures), capped by "establish self-checks / meditation."

TL;DR

  • Lines up four primary 2020-21 college funding targets: OSU app, Renaissance, Ford, JCEF — the four scholarships that ended up funding Yahir's OSU freshman year.
  • Sketches a stocks-investment workflow: "price points, scenarios and thesis's, solidified watchlist with alerts" — formalizing the watchlist work begun in the March COVID balance sheet pass.
  • Names two income streams: Amazon (with a "quarantine business for dad" angle and a wholesale route) and marketing (local DoorDash, SEO enhancement) — earliest crisp articulation of bridge income thinking.
  • "Dad on DoorDash + Grubhub and Postmates??" — Yahir prototyping gig-work onboarding for his father; pairs with the "FOR DAD LOAN" EIDL paperwork eight months earlier.
  • "Completing Adventures before farewell — 18 bday Ever trip, foreign country visit, walking a marathon" — pre-OSU bucket list; the foreign-country line foreshadows the 2021 destination shortlist and Shanghai study abroad.

Pattern observed

Locks in the senior-year four-pillar stack: scholarships + stocks + earned income + adventures-before-launch. The structure is template-shaped — four columns, each with sub-bullets, each readable as a project board. Same skeleton recurs in the "master plan" life-document phrase Yahir uses through 2022. The closing "establish self-checks / meditation" is also the earliest meditation-as-discipline mention in the corpus — predates the therapy entries by years.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir actually walk a marathon before leaving Grants Pass?
  • Did the dad-on-DoorDash idea ever execute, or just stay on the list?

Related

  • osu
  • renaissance scholarship
  • ford family foundation
  • jcef
  • bridge income strategies
